title: sorbus aria subsp. carpinifolia
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. carpinifolia, also known as the wild service tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it is also used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sorbus aria subsp. carpinifolia include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable herbal remedy for promoting overall well-being. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves made from its bark, leaves, and seeds, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
